title: centranthera tranquebarica
intro: Centranthera tranquebarica, also known as the Indian valerian or Kateli, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, commonly found in the wild and used in traditional medicine. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its chemical compounds. The health benefits of Centranthera tranquebarica include its potential to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and other sleep disorders, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and fever. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used in Ayurvedic and traditional medicine practices.
